contract.goal: Ship linear-loom task comment <slug> --task=<composed-key> --body=<text> (with --body-file=<path> alt). Posts a comment on the Linear Sub-Issue whose composed_key matches --task, returns {slug, task: {composed_key, linear_id}, comment: {id, url}} as JSON. The primitive that U2 (checkin write) will compose against.
acceptance_criteria: New plugins/linear-loom/cli/lib/comments.ts: createComment({client, issueId, body}) wraps Linear commentCreate mutation; throws LinearLoomError('comment-create-failed', ...) on success=false or null comment.

New plugins/linear-loom/cli/verbs/task.ts: taskComment async + TASK_VERBS map ({comment: taskComment}). Parses positional <slug> + --task + --body XOR --body-file + --pretty. Resolves marker → auth → fetchLinearState → composed_key lookup → createComment → emits structured JSON.
Error codes: missing-slug, missing-task-id, missing-body, conflicting-body, body-file-unreadable, task-not-found, task-target-is-milestone, comment-create-failed.
Wired into plugins/linear-loom/cli/linear-loom.ts VERB_HANDLERS as task: TASK_VERBS.
Tests: comments.ts +4 (success, success=false → error, null-comment → error, request-body shape); task.ts +12 (all 8 error codes + 2 success paths Task/Batch + --body-file injection + --pretty).
npm test → 838 passing (was 822 after U4; +16 net).
rules_applied: DESIGN.md § 7 (checkins post as comments on Linear Sub-Issues; task comment is the primitive verb the checkin-write substrate composes on).
DESIGN.md § 12.7 (composed_key is the cross-substrate identity layer; --task accepts composed_key, never linear_id).
disqualifiers: Pre-rendering structured checkin JSON to markdown — that's U2 (checkin write) building on this primitive.
Editing or deleting existing comments — creation only in v1.
Threading replies (parent comment IDs) — flat comment posting only.
Composed-key prefix matching — exact match only.
Commenting on Phases / Milestones — Linear's API doesn't support it; reject with task-target-is-milestone.
Bulk operations or stdin streaming of bodies — one comment per invocation.
inputs: plugins/linear-loom/cli/verbs/tasks.ts (existing verb shape — used as template)

actions: Wrote cli/lib/comments.ts: createComment({client, issueId, body}) wrapping Linear's commentCreate mutation. Throws LinearLoomError('comment-create-failed', ...) when success !== true OR comment === null — covers both failure shapes. Returns {id, url}.

Wrote cli/verbs/task.ts: taskComment async verb. Parses slug + --task + --body XOR --body-file + --pretty. Resolves marker / auth / fetchLinearState in sequence. composed_key.trim() lookup against by_composed_key Map (exact match). Phase nodes rejected with task-target-is-milestone — Linear's API doesn't support comments on ProjectMilestones. Emits {slug, task: {composed_key, linear_id}, comment: {id, url}} on success.

Wired task namespace into linear-loom.ts VERB_HANDLERS as task: TASK_VERBS. CLI dispatch: task → missing-verb with candidates ['comment']; task comment → missing-slug; task comment <slug> → missing-task-id; task comment <slug> --task=<key> → missing-body.

Wrote 16 unit tests: comments.test.ts (success returns {id, url}, success=false throws, null-comment throws, request body shape carries issueId + body); task.test.ts (8 error-code branches + Task success + Batch success + --body-file via injected reader + comment-create-failed bubble + --pretty pretty-print).
Verified: npm test → 838 passing across 58 files (was 822 after Phase 5 U4; +16). Panel (contract-fit + naming + test-unit): all 3 approved on first run; zero findings.

changes_since_previous: First unit of Phase 6 (Manual write-back verbs), opening branch off main now that Phase 5 closed. Phase 6 sliced per-verb at operator request (matching Phase 5's stacked-PR pattern); 4 units total: U1 task comment (this PR), U2 checkin write, U3 phase update, U4 events read.
notes_for_pr: task vs tasks: the singular `task` namespace operates on one Sub-Issue identified by composed_key (`task comment`); the plural `tasks` namespace from Phase 5 operates over the full PLAN.md reconcile set (`tasks generate`). One-letter-difference identifiers are grep-hostile (`rg TASK_VERBS` matches both); the file paths (`verbs/task.ts` vs `verbs/tasks.ts`) differentiate at import sites. Reviewable; preserved deliberately because the semantic distinction is principled.

Phase 6 has no Linear-Milestone-comment story because Linear's API doesn't expose a comment surface on ProjectMilestones — only on Issues. `task-target-is-milestone` is the clean rejection. § 7's intent is met for Batches and Tasks; Phase-level discussion belongs in the Linear Project's main discussion thread (which linear-loom doesn't touch in v1).

Comments are append-only on Linear's side, so re-running this verb with the same body creates a duplicate. The substrate doesn't deduplicate — legitimate operator use cases include posting the same status twice, and deduplication policy would be a stronger constraint than warranted at the primitive level. Captured in the verb's leading comment.

Empty-string --body-file path (`--body-file=''`) routes to missing-body rather than body-file-unreadable, because the empty-string check (`bodyFile !== ''`) treats it as 'no flag.' Defensible (empty path is indistinguishable from absent), noted by the contract-fit evaluator as an advisory observation. Could be tightened to body-file-unreadable in a follow-up if anyone hits it.
scope-shift signals: zero — phase start, no whiteboard contradictions, no evaluator findings about plan structure, no user comment proposing plan changes. Clean continuation of Phase 6.

Substrate follow-up still standing: bin/guild derive-panel returns 'panel-spec-unreadable (using fallback)' under the marketplace-installed CLI; used `node plugins/guild/cli/guild.ts` locally throughout. Same gap captured in U4's checkin and the agents-repo memory file.
